creating-folders-and-files.label: Creating Folders and Files
creating-folders-and-files.summary: Create useful folder structures with mkdir, mkdir -p, and touch.

sc-mkdir-purpose.title: What `mkdir` creates
sc-mkdir-purpose.hint: Create parent folders before files and copy every required path exactly.

help.concept: `mkdir` creates folders, `mkdir -p` creates missing parent paths, and `touch` creates an empty file.
help.hint_1: Create parent folders before files and copy every required path exactly.
help.hint_2: Use targeted `ls` commands to verify each new folder or file before checking the answer. The current task asks: What does `mkdir` create?

mkdir-one-folder.title: Create one folder with `mkdir`
mkdir-one-folder.bodyMarkdown:
`mkdir` creates a directory in the current location. The command shape is `mkdir folder-name`.

```bash
mkdir reading-list
ls
```

After the command, `reading-list` should appear in the listing. `mkdir` does not move you into the new folder, and it will not quietly replace a folder that already exists. Choose a clear name, create it once, and verify before building anything inside it.

mkdir-p-nested.title: Create nested folders with `mkdir -p`
mkdir-p-nested.bodyMarkdown:
When a structure needs several levels, `mkdir -p` can create missing parent folders and the final folder in one command.

```bash
mkdir -p travel-pack/tickets travel-pack/maps
```

This creates `travel-pack` first, then the two folders inside it. Without `-p`, a nested command fails when the parent does not exist. The option is helpful, but still verify the exact names with targeted `ls` commands so a spelling mistake does not become an unnoticed second folder.

touch-files.title: Create empty files with `touch`
touch-files.bodyMarkdown:
`touch` creates an empty file when the named file does not exist. It is useful for placeholders that will receive content later.

```bash
touch research/links.txt
ls research
```

The parent folder must already exist. If `research` is missing, create it before running `touch`. An empty file is still a real workspace item, so give it a meaningful name rather than creating anonymous clutter. The next lesson will replace or append text inside files with `echo` and redirection.
